title: Case study
logical_links: This is a minimal section-divider slide containing only the heading 'Case study' centered on an otherwise empty canvas. It introduces the case-study block of the deck (slides 42-49), signalling a transition from strategy/proposal content to proof-of-work examples. There are no supporting images or body text; the single large heading is the entire payload, setting the reading context for the slides that follow.

title: Hunter Boots

children.text: We operate social media account for an iconic UK fashion brand. We operated the brand more more than 2 years and help Hunter to achieve sales growth of over 30%.
During the 6.18 Little Red Book campaign working with 40 +  influencers and over 7 million impression
Worth of free promotion received from KOL on Little Red Book  by gifting 4k USD products per month
Free impression on WeChat and Weibo via product gifting

images.description: The HUNTER wordmark logo: black bold letters on a cream background, framed by a red border.
A young woman sitting outdoors on grass against a blue sky, wearing tall red Hunter rubber boots, posing with a hand to her head; an influencer-style lifestyle product shot.

A Xiaohongshu (Little Red Book) post screenshot by user '易梦玲' captioned 'ootd | 最近喜欢蓝色', showing a woman in blue Hunter boots relaxing in a hammock by a lake; the post shows 5.1万 likes, 4862 saves and 960 comments.

logical_links: The slide is a case study proving WalktheChat's social-media operating capability for Hunter Boots. The 'Hunter Boots' heading and its body paragraph (2+ years operating the account, 30%+ sales growth) establish the engagement, reinforced by the Hunter logo top-right. Beneath, a four-column KPI band quantifies results: No. 2 top performing brand in a 6.18 RED campaign (40+ influencers, 7M impressions), 5+ top-tier celebrities via gifting, 20,000+ USD of earned promotion, and 3+ million free impressions. The two photos on the left visually substantiate the influencer/KOL gifting tactic referenced in the KPIs - a lifestyle shot of red boots and an actual RED post with concrete engagement numbers, linking the claimed earned media to real social proof. Reading flow goes left photos -> heading/body upper right, then top-to-bottom into the KPI row.

title: Dirk Nowitzki X Bauerfeind

text_hierarchy.text: 1 paid WeChat KOL, 7 un-paid* WeChat KOLs, 30 un-paid* Weibo KOLs*
Signature on posters for lucky draw winners
* Un-paid KOL receive around 1000RMB worth of Bauerfeind product and 4 KOLs also has the chance to meet with Dirk

images.description: Full-bleed key visual of basketball player Dirk Nowitzki standing with arms outstretched, holding a Spalding basketball and wearing a Bauerfeind arm sleeve, against a dark grey studio gradient with the white Bauerfeind logo.
Near-identical key visual of Dirk Nowitzki with arms outstretched holding a basketball and wearing a Bauerfeind arm sleeve, Bauerfeind logo at bottom; layered as a second background image.
Mobile livestream screenshot showing Dirk Nowitzki standing beside a fan, both wearing Dallas Mavericks #41 jerseys, in a Bauerfeind-branded backdrop room (lucky-draw winner meeting Dirk).
Mobile livestream screenshot of Dirk Nowitzki seated at a table being interviewed by a man, in a Bauerfeind-branded room (basketball KOL interview).
Bauerfeind-branded image of a man signing a row of life-size cardboard standees, with Chinese caption '德克签名人形立牌' (Dirk-signed life-size standees).
Mobile livestream screenshot of Dirk Nowitzki standing at a table presenting on stage in a Bauerfeind-branded room, with live chat overlays and gift animations (the live-streaming event).

logical_links: This case study showcases a celebrity-athlete campaign: Dirk Nowitzki x Bauerfeind during his Beijing visit. The full-bleed athlete key visual forms the backdrop, immediately tying the slide to the Bauerfeind brand and its NBA ambassador. Across the top sits a row of four mobile screenshots, each labelled by a bold caption directly above/below it - 'Live-streaming', 'Lucky-draw winner meet with Dirk', 'Basketball KOL interview' and 'Signature on posters for lucky draw winners' - so each image documents one concrete campaign activity. The lower-left text block lists the campaign's quantified results (1 paid + 37 un-paid KOLs, 3M impressions, 4,000 comments, 3,000 lucky-draw participants and new followers), and the bottom-right footnote clarifies the 'un-paid' KOL model (gifted ~1000RMB product, 4 got to meet Dirk). Logic flows: athlete visual establishes the asset -> screenshots prove the activations -> results block and footnote explain the cost-efficient KOL mechanism that produced them.

title: HEYTEA AI Collaboration Video: Boosting Engagement and Likes

text_hierarchy.text: HEYTEA AI Collaboration Video: Boosting Engagement and Likes

images.description: HEYTEA x Marimekko campaign product shot: two HEYTEA paper cups (one green, one white) printed with a stylised Unikko-flower figure drinking tea, set on a beige backdrop surrounded by white gardenia flowers and moss, with 'HEYTEA marimekko' lockup and a Xiaohongshu watermark (小红书号: 2683130197).
An empty white smartphone mockup frame (iPhone-style with notch), used as a placeholder/embed for a video.
An empty white smartphone mockup frame (iPhone-style with notch), used as a placeholder/embed for a video.

logical_links: The slide demonstrates an AI-driven content production case for HEYTEA's Marimekko (Unikko) collaboration, with the title framing the outcome: AI collaboration video boosting engagement and likes. The campaign product key visual fills the background to establish the HEYTEA x Marimekko context, while two phone mockup frames act as embedded-video players: the left phone is labelled 'HEYTEA CGI Hybrid video / Click to Watch' and the right phone 'UNIKKO 60th Anniversary / Click to Watch'. Floating KPI badges quantify each video's performance - '329% Engagement', 'Red 10K+ Likes Video' and '+7000 Instagram Likes' - positioned near the phones they describe, so each clickable video is paired with its result metric. Reading flow: title claim -> two playable videos in phone frames -> KPI badges that prove the engagement uplift.

title: Marimekko Unikko 60 Years Events in Shanghai

text_hierarchy.text: Marimekko Unikko 60 Years Events in Shanghai

children.text: of organic posts and under comment bar

images.description: An outdoor fashion runway/showcase on the colourful painted steps of the IAPM mall in Shanghai, with many models posed wearing vibrant Marimekko Unikko-print garments.
A bright pink Marimekko pop-up interior with large Unikko poppy-print artworks on the wall, a white tulip table with red-and-white cups, and an Unikko-print floor; a styled event/experience space.

logical_links: A RED-style case study for Marimekko's Unikko 60th anniversary events in Shanghai, headed by the 'CASE STUDY' label and bold title. The two left photos show the physical activations - a Unikko-print runway on the IAPM mall steps and a colourful pink pop-up interior - giving visual evidence of the on-the-ground events. On the right, the upper KPI table quantifies digital results (2,700,000+ impressions, 13,000+ engagements, 400+ UGCs & photo comments), while the lower 'First' table highlights two innovation milestones: first local-produced digital creation using AI/CG/shooting, and first Trunk Show livestream on RED. The flow connects the offline events (photos) to their online amplification metrics (top-right) and to the firsts that made the campaign distinctive (bottom-right).

title: Marimekko 10-year in China Exhibition in Beijing

text_hierarchy.text: Marimekko 10-year in China Exhibition in Beijing

logical_links: A companion case-study slide to slide 45, this one covering Marimekko's 10-year-in-China exhibition in Beijing. The 'CASE STUDY' label and bold title sit at the top left, and the entire right side is a single KPI table stacking five results pairs: 3,000,000+ impressions, 10,600+ engagements, 40+ KOLs invited for the grand opening, 4,200+ participants within 14 days, and 250+ UGCs on RED. With no images, the slide relies purely on the metric ladder to demonstrate the scale and reach of the exhibition, each big number reinforced by a small descriptive caption beneath it.

title: AGATHA : Planned and executed the brand’s 50th anniversary exhibition, supporting a successful brand image upgrade.

text_hierarchy.text: AGATHA : Planned and executed the brand’s 50th anniversary exhibition, supporting a successful brand image upgrade.

children.text: Assisted the brand in elevating core consumer age group and spending power through a successful brand image upgrade.
Integrated online and offline campaigns to boost brand visibility and reinforce brand positioning.
Supported the brand in enhancing the full-funnel strategy from exposure to conversion during the campaign period.
fans on AGATHA’s brand account within 1 month
engagement growth compared to last month

images.description: A full-height fashion editorial photo of an Asian woman in a black wide-brim hat and deep-V denim outfit, wearing layered gold AGATHA coin necklaces and gold earrings, looking up in a lush green garden; the 'AGATHA' logotype is overlaid in white.
The AGATHA PARIS wordmark logo in black on a white background.

logical_links: This case study presents WalktheChat's work on AGATHA's 50th-anniversary exhibition and brand-image upgrade. The bold title states the engagement and outcome, with 'Case study: AGATHA' as a subtitle beneath it. The left column is structured into two labelled sections: 'Context' (three bullets on elevating the consumer age group/spending power, integrating online+offline campaigns, and enhancing the full-funnel strategy) and 'Results' (a paired list of 3,500 new fans in one month, 103%+ engagement growth, and 60+ KOL posts), with the AGATHA Paris logo anchoring the lower left. The full-height editorial photo on the right carries the brand's aspirational image upgrade visually, and the caption '50th Anniversary Campaign Engagement: 54K' at its base ties the hero visual back to a headline campaign metric. Flow: title/subtitle -> Context rationale -> Results metrics, with the right-hand image and its 54K caption providing the visual proof of the upgraded brand image.

title: AGATHA 50 Year Events: In partnership for the 50th anniversary, we provided the campaign’s creative theme, completed the online planning and execution, and supported the offline creative concept.

text_hierarchy.text: AGATHA 50 Year Events: In partnership for the 50th anniversary, we provided the campaign’s creative theme, completed the online planning and execution, and supported the offline creative concept.

children.text: Supported by the French Trade and Investment Agency
Key guests from the fashion industry attended
including event recap and brand pronunciation showcase

images.description: Exterior of a white colonial-style villa entrance hosting the AGATHA event, with an AGATHA Paris pillar sign and blue floral decorations; a logo of the French trade agency appears at the bottom.
Interior exhibition scene: a woman in a navy jumpsuit gestures beside a large outline of AGATHA's Scottie-dog mascot, in front of a wall collage with 'AGATHA PARIS' and 'DEPUIS 1974' graphics.
A large blue-and-white draped fabric art installation/sculpture displayed in the villa's exhibition hall, with an AGATHA exhibition poster on the left wall.
A group of seven well-dressed guests raising champagne glasses for a toast in front of a white decorative tree at the AGATHA anniversary event.

logical_links: A continuation of the AGATHA 50th-anniversary case study, this slide details WalktheChat's scope (creative theme, online planning/execution, offline creative-concept support) in the title across the top. A 2x2 grid of event photos on the left fills the slide with visual proof of the offline exhibition - the villa entrance, the Scottie-dog mascot wall, a draped fabric art installation, and a champagne toast among industry guests - illustrating the staged brand experience. The right-hand KPI table quantifies deliverables and reach: 20+ social posts on WeChat Channel and RED, the First offline brand exhibition in China (backed by the French Trade and Investment Agency), 100+ fashion-industry guests, multiple design materials, 740+ photo assets and 2 video assets. The photos in the grid directly correspond to the table's claims (e.g., the guest toast supports '100+ key guests'; the venue/installation shots support the 'First offline exhibition'). Tiny footer labels 'AGATHA' and '1. Event Overview' mark this as the first section of the AGATHA event recap.

title: DANIEL ARSHAM

text_hierarchy.text: We build an E-commerce infrastructure supporting a famous artist with flash-sales campaigns
Flash-sales campaign was sold out within 1 hour
Handled a huge amount of consecutive request
Support with customer service for refunds

images.description: Full-bleed portrait of artist Daniel Arsham in a white coat, dark cap and round glasses, standing behind a white plinth that holds one of his eroded/relic-style sculptures, against a sculptural white wavy topographic wall.

logical_links: This case study highlights WalktheChat's e-commerce/technical infrastructure work for the artist Daniel Arsham. His full-bleed studio portrait serves as the slide background and brand context, with the 'DANIEL ARSHAM' title overlaid top-left. The left-side text column explains the engagement - building an e-commerce infrastructure to support flash-sales campaigns - and then lists four concrete capabilities/outcomes as body lines: sold out within 1 hour, bot filtering, handling massive consecutive traffic, and refund customer service. The logic moves from who (Arsham portrait + name) to what (the heading) to proof points (the bulleted technical achievements), positioning WalktheChat as able to handle high-pressure, high-traffic drops for a premium artist collaboration.
